title: goodenia integerrima
intro: Goodenia integerrima, also known as the 'Gnudju' or 'Hairy Goodenia', is a shrub-like plant native to Australia, belonging to the Goodeniaceae family. Traditionally, Goodenia integerrima has been used in Aboriginal medicine, boasting medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Goodenia integerrima can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, allowing for a range of applications in folk medicine.
